Vol. 01 — Contents
i.

An Introduction

A book about holding on to the moments that mattered.

This is the reference book for the Moment Lab brand — who we are, how we speak, and how we show up in the world. It's written to be used: by the founder, by collaborators, by anyone creating something with the Moment Lab name attached to it.

Keep it close. Return to it often. And when in doubt, ask whether what you're making feels like it belongs to a brand whose whole reason for being is the careful preservation of joy.

p. 03 — Story
01.

Chapter One

The Story.

The best parts of any event deserve more than a phone snap.

A craft, not a supplier.

Moment Lab was founded on a quiet rebellion against what the photobooth industry had become — inflatable frames, tacky props, printouts already curling before the night was over. We had seen, across a decade in event management, what made a celebration truly unforgettable. It was never the cheapest option. It was the carefully chosen one.

So we built something different. Pro-grade printing that holds its colour for a lifetime. Backdrops curated like a fashion shoot — never hired from a catalogue. An attendant present at every event, because a premium experience is never self-service. And a visual language that belongs at the most considered weddings, corporate launches and milestone celebrations in the North East.

The name is the promise. Moment — for what happens. Lab — for the craft that preserves it.

Founded 2026 · Newcastle · Serving Newcastle, Northumberland, Durham, Tees Valley and beyond · momentlab.co.uk

p. 04 — Pillars
02.

Chapter Two

Five pillars.
One promise.

Every decision we make — from booking a date to designing a print template — is measured against these five pillars. If a choice doesn't strengthen one, we reconsider it.

i.
Craft

Pro-grade prints. Curated backdrops. Equipment chosen for performance, not pricing. Everything we produce should hold up to close inspection.

ii.
Warmth

A person — not a booth — behind every event. Over ten years of event experience translates to guests who feel looked after, not processed.

iii.
Discretion

Premium service doesn't shout. Our setup looks considered, not cluttered. Our brand presence is felt, never forced.

iv.
Memory

We are not in the entertainment business. We are in the memory business. The keepsake is the product; everything else is how we deliver it.

v.
Restraint

Black, gold, a single considered script. We resist the temptation to add and add. Quiet design ages better than loud design.

p. 05 — Logo
03.

Chapter Three

The logo,
in full.

The Moment Lab mark pairs a handwritten script — for the spontaneity of a captured moment — with a geometric sans-serif word, lab, set wide to evoke the precision of a darkroom. The two halves are inseparable. Never use one without the other.

“Moment” · Gistesy   ·   “LAB” · Josefin Sans   ·   Both free via Canva

Primary — gilt on obsidian preferred
Mono — obsidian on paper
Gilt on smoke
Bone on obsidian — occasional
Usage Priority

The gilt-on-obsidian lockup is our signature — use it wherever possible, especially for printed material, social profile artwork, signage, and any application seen alongside an event. The mono variants exist for situations where colour cannot be reproduced faithfully, not as creative alternatives.

File Library

The logo is supplied as:

  • · moment-lab-primary.svg — gilt on obsidian (full lockup)
  • · moment-lab-gold-transparent.svg — gilt, no background
  • · moment-lab-black-transparent.svg — mono black
  • · moment-lab-bone-transparent.svg — bone/cream

Always use the supplied SVG files. Never recreate, trace, or type-set the logo from scratch.

p. 05b — Extended Lockup
03.

Section 3.2

The extended lockup.

For physical environments where a passing guest must immediately understand what we do — table covers, pop-up banners, A-frame signs at venue entrances — we use an extended mark that adds the descriptor Photobooth Rental beneath the primary lockup. Set in the same typefaces, the same gilt, the same proportions.

Extended Primary — gilt on obsidian  ·  for physical signage, pop-ups, apparel

Extended Secondary — gilt on bone  ·  for paper collateral, light venues

When to use

Use the extended lockup when the brand appears in isolation in a physical space, away from the website or Instagram feed — where the guest has seconds to register what the setup is for. Table covers, pull-up banners, A-frame signs, aprons, venue-entrance signage.

When not to use

Never use the extended lockup for digital surfaces — website, Instagram, email signature — where the primary lockup is already contextualised. Never use it on small applications under 80 mm wide — the descriptor becomes illegible. Default to the primary whenever the primary will suffice.

File Library — Extended
  • · moment-lab-extended-obsidian.png — gilt on obsidian, 2560×1440
  • · moment-lab-extended-bone.png — gilt on bone, 2560×1440

Source artwork designed in Canva using Gistesy (script) and Josefin Sans (LAB & descriptor).

p. 06 — Clear Space & Sizing
03.

Section 3.2

Room to breathe.

The logo needs space. Give it the same measure of quiet you'd give a good portrait on a gallery wall. Nothing — no text, image, edge, or decorative element — should enter the clear-space zone.

x x x x
The Rule

x = the height of the word lab.

Use the height of the word LAB as your unit of measure. Leave at least one x of clear space on every side of the lockup. More is always welcome.

Minimum Sizes

Digital: never below 80 px wide.
Print: never below 25 mm wide.
Below these sizes the script detail becomes illegible and the logo loses its integrity.

Scale Examples
Full · 25mm+
Medium
Small
Too small — avoid
p. 07 — Misuse
03.

Section 3.3

Don't do this.

A consistent mark is what separates a brand from a business. Resist the urge to adapt, tweak, decorate, or “make it pop”. Respecting these rules is how the Moment Lab logo builds recognition over time.

Do not stretch
Do not rotate
Do not recolour
Do not add effects
Do not place on pattern
Do not use low contrast
Also Avoid

· Reconstructing the mark from fonts that are not the original.
· Using only the script or only “LAB”.
· Adding outlines, drop shadows, or gradients.
· Placing over busy or low-contrast photographs.
· Using non-brand colours such as red, blue or bright gold.

Over Imagery

The logo may be placed over a photograph only when the image is dark, quiet, and low in contrast — and only in gilt or bone. If the composition fights the logo, the logo loses. When in doubt, place it on a solid obsidian block instead.

p. 08 — Colour
04.

Chapter Four

Obsidian & gilt.
A lifetime palette.

Black and gold, rendered warm. Our palette is built to age — it has no trends to outlive. The primary two do most of the work; the supporting tones exist for breathing room and paper applications.

Primary Obsidian.
HEX  #0A0A0A
CMYK  0 / 0 / 0 / 100
PANTONE  Black 6 C
Accent Gilt.
#C9A84C
PMS 7555
Paper Bone.
#F4EEE2
PMS 9184
Support Smoke.
#1A1814
PMS 419 C
Supporting Tones

Used sparingly, for gradient depth, UI states, and photographic treatment. They are never used as headline colours.

Coal #2A2823
Tobacco #3A2F1A
Soft Gilt #D9BC68
Paper #EAE2D0
Usage Ratio

A rough guide — not a formula. Obsidian leads, bone rests, gilt punctuates. If gilt is ever more than a tenth of the page, pull it back.

60% Obsidian
30% Bone
10%
Obsidian · Bone · Gilt
The Forbidden

Never neon. Never pastel. Never cool-toned black (our obsidian is warm). Never bright yellow — our gilt is aged, a little dusky, never sunny.

p. 09 — Typography
05.

Chapter Five

Two typefaces.
A third for moments.

Our typography mirrors the logo architecture: a refined serif for editorial breath, a precise geometric sans for structure, and — used sparingly — a script that recalls the handwritten Moment mark itself.

A note on the logo

The script “Moment” is set in Gistesy. “LAB” beneath it is set in Josefin Sans, the same typeface that carries our body copy below. Both are available free through Canva and Google Fonts.

Moments, held.
Cormorant Garamond Display / Editorial Light 300 · Regular 400 · Italic Google Fonts · Free

Cormorant carries our headlines, display copy, pull-quotes, and all printed collateral. Use the light weight for hero type, regular for subheadings, italic for emphasis and ornament. Tracking should be tight (−2%) on display sizes.

Newcastle · Est. 2026
Josefin Sans Body / Structural Light 300 · Regular 400 · Medium 500 · Semi 600 Google Fonts & Canva · Free

Josefin Sans handles body copy, captions, labels, buttons, and anything uppercase-with-tracking. Its geometric bones match the “LAB” half of the logomark exactly — the same typeface is set wide beneath the script. For labels and eyebrows, always uppercase with letter-spacing between 0.2em and 0.35em.

Hold the moment
Dancing Script Accent only Regular 400 · Medium 500 Google Fonts · Free

A handwritten accent, used rarely and never in blocks. Reserved for single phrases — a wedding couple's names, a thank-you on a print, the occasional Instagram flourish. In gilt, never in black. If it's tempting to use it twice on the same piece, remove it once.

The Hierarchy
H1 · Display
Moments, held.
H2 · Section
The Story.
H3 · Sub
A craft, not a supplier.
H4 · Lead
Pro-grade prints, thoughtfully made.
Body
Every event begins with a conversation. We learn the brief, agree the details, and turn up ready — tuxedo black booth, curated backdrop, warm prints in guests' hands within seconds. The goal is always the same: a night your guests remember and a set of keepsakes that outlive it.
Caption / Label
Est. 2026 — Newcastle & North East
p. 10 — Voice
06.

Chapter Six

How we sound.

Plainspoken luxury. Warm, never gushing. British to the bone.

Our personality, in one sentence.

Moment Lab speaks like a confident, well-travelled friend who happens to run a beautiful small business — someone who knows the craft inside out but never needs to tell you so.

We favour clarity over cleverness, specifics over superlatives, and quiet confidence over excitable claims. We are not here to convince anyone that we are “the best” — we are here to be the right fit for a particular kind of event and a particular kind of host.

Principles
  1. i.  Always UK English. Enquiry, not inquiry. Bespoke, not custom.
  2. ii.  Specific over general. “Pro-grade instant prints”, not “great photos”.
  3. iii.  No exclamation marks. No emojis. No all-caps shouting.
  4. iv.  Warm, not cute. Refined, not stiff. Short, not clipped.
  5. v.  Say it once. Repetition dilutes elegance.
Tonal Dials

The voice stays constant. The tone adjusts to context. Think of these as faders on a mixing desk — we push some up and others down depending on what we're writing.

Warm ← Reserved

Warmer for weddings · reserved for corporate

Poetic ← Direct

Poetic for hero copy · direct for FAQs

Personal ← Formal

“I” in About · “we” in brand copy

Confident ← Humble

Confident about craft · humble about self

p. 11 — Do & Don't
06.

Section 6.2

Do this. Not that.

The fastest way to learn the voice is to see it in action. Below are paired examples — same idea, two renderings. The left column is Moment Lab. The right is the common photobooth trap we work hard to avoid.

Do — Moment Lab

Premium photo experiences, thoughtfully made.

Pro-grade instant prints. Curated backdrops. Real keepsakes.

Bespoke photo experiences for weddings, celebrations and brand events across the North East.

Enquire about your date.

We'd be delighted to hear about your plans.

Two backdrops. Chosen for reason, not variety.

Don't — Generic

AMAZING photobooths for your special day!!! 🎉🎉🎉

The BEST selfie booth in Newcastle — hire us now!

Photobooths for hire — weddings, parties, corporate & more.

Click here to book instantly!!

DM us for a quote babes 💖

10+ backdrops to choose from — something for everyone!

Word Preferences

We prefer
Enquiry · Bespoke · Considered · Host ·
Keepsake · Evening · Occasion · Capture · Craft ·
Curated · Understated · In due course · Gather

We avoid
Inquiry · Custom · Amazing · Vendor · Client ·
Souvenir · Night · Party · Snap · Service ·
Lots of · Luxe · ASAP · Reach out · Family

Punctuation & Formatting

Em-dashes are welcome — they give copy room to breathe. Semi-colons are fine when you know why you're using one. Ellipses, exclamation marks, and emoji are not part of the brand voice. Capitalise event-type words naturally in sentences; treat “Moment Lab” as a proper noun always.

p. 11 — Sample Copy
06.

Section 6.3

The voice, in use.

A working library of copy across the places Moment Lab shows up. Use these verbatim or as patterns for new writing.

Homepage — Hero

Premium photo experiences for weddings, celebrations and brand events across Newcastle and the North East.

The best moments aren't just captured. They're kept.

Instagram — Bio

Moment Lab · Newcastle & North East
Weddings · Celebrations · Brand Events
Premium photobooth · Pro-grade prints
↓ Enquire below

About — Short

Moment Lab was built on a simple idea: that the best parts of any event deserve more than a phone snap. They deserve to be printed, kept, and remembered. Founded after over a decade in event management, we create photo experiences for the hosts who care about every detail.

Enquiry — Auto-reply

Thank you for enquiring with Moment Lab. Your message is in, and we'll be in touch within 48 hours with a bespoke package proposal. In the meantime, please don't hesitate to share any early thoughts on the evening — we love hearing the story behind each booking.

Services — One-liner

Pro-grade prints. Curated backdrops. An attendant at every event. Live digital gallery. Bespoke on-screen design. Set-up and breakdown included.

Sign-off

Warmly,
Moment Lab
Newcastle & North East

p. 12 — Photography
07.

Chapter Seven

A photographer's eye.

Our own photography — of the booth, the backdrops, the events — should look like editorial fashion or fine-dining imagery. Low light, warm shadow, gold glimmer, honest texture. Guests caught mid-laughter, never mid-pose. Detail shots of prints, linen, flowers, hands.

hero Low light · warm tungsten
Print detail · texture
Mood — obsidian & shadow
Gilt moment
Backdrop · portrait
Always

· Warm, tungsten-leaning colour temperature.
· Generous negative space.
· Unposed candids over set-ups.
· Close-ups of prints in hands.
· Atmosphere over information.

Occasionally

· Flash, when it serves drama.
· Black & white for portraits only.
· The booth as hero — if the venue supports it.
· Overhead detail flatlays for launches.

Never

· Cool white or fluorescent light.
· Heavy saturation or HDR.
· Stock photography of strangers.
· Props piled on tables.
· Children's faces without written consent.

Print Collateral — Welcome Card

Included in every event box: a 105 × 148 mm card introducing the attendant, outlining the run-of-show, and thanking the host by name. Printed on the same stock as the business card reverse.

A word of thanks.

We're delighted to be part of your evening. Your attendant tonight is Sarah — find her by the booth, or ask the venue team to point her out. All digital copies will reach you within 48 hours.

Warmly, Moment Lab Photobooth
p. 14 — Branded Items
08.

Section 8.2

On the night itself.

The moment a guest walks into the venue, Moment Lab should already be introducing itself — quietly, legibly, consistently. Below are the approved branded items that carry the Extended Lockup into the physical world.

Moments, held.
momentlab.co.uk

Pop-up Banner — 850 × 2000 mm

Retractable roll-up banner. Placed at venue entrances or alongside the booth in larger spaces. Extended lockup centred upper-two-thirds.

Table Cover — 6 ft · 1800 × 760 mm

Fitted matte-black polyester cover for all trestle tables. Logo centre-front, no branding on sides or back. Steamed before every event.

the evening of
Rachel & Tom
Photobooth · this way →

A-Frame Welcome Sign — A1

Personalised per event. Couple or brand name set in Cormorant Italic; arrow updated per venue orientation.

attendant

Attendant Apron — gilt embroidery

Matte-black canvas apron. Gilt thread embroidery on the left chest — the extended lockup positioned over the heart. Never printed, never full-size.

Your gallery, instantly.
Scan to view
Evans · Thornhall · 2026

QR Gallery Card — 105 × 148 mm

Placed beside the booth on the night. One QR code per event linking guests to their private live gallery. Personalised per booking.

Print Specifications

Banners & covers: direct-to-substrate digital print, matte finish only. Never gloss, never laminate.
Signage: 5 mm foamex or foam-core board. White edge, cut flush.
Embroidery: Madeira Classic 1024 gold thread, 0.4 mm satin stitch.
Paper collateral: 350 gsm uncoated, warm white.

Other Approved Items

· Delivery crate sticker — 100 mm square, gilt on kraft
· Attendant name badge — brass, engraved, magnetic fix
· Thank-you card insert — A6, bone, handwritten per event
· Cable ties / gaffer — matte black only, no branded tape
· Equipment flight cases — stencil logo, gilt paint

Any new physical item must use the extended lockup, a matte finish, and be approved before order.

Moments, held.
